Flagler County FHA Loans

FHA loans in Flagler County, Florida

FHA loans across Flagler County with as little as 3.5% down. Here's the county's 2026 FHA loan limit, why Palm Coast and the area suit FHA buyers, and how to get pre-approved.

Flagler County Home Loans

FHA loans in Flagler County, Florida

Flagler County, on Florida's northeast coast between St. Augustine and Daytona Beach, is dominated by the master-planned city of Palm Coast. It's an affordable, fast-growing market with extensive new construction, drawing first-time buyers, retirees, and remote workers. The FHA loan limit comfortably covers the typical Flagler County home, so 3.5% down works well across most of the county.

Flagler County FHA limit (1-unit)$541,287

2026 single-family

Minimum down3.5%

With a 580+ score

Min credit score580

500–579 with 10% down

Property types1–4 units

Owner-occupied

What the FHA limit means in Flagler County

With the 2026 FHA floor of $541,287 and FHA's 3.5% minimum down, you can finance a Flagler County home priced up to roughly $560,000 (your loan covers the price minus your down payment). In Flagler's affordable market, that covers the vast majority of homes, including much of the new construction. A handful of Florida counties are set higher, so we confirm Flagler County's exact figure with HUD.

Palm Coast growth and coastal value

Palm Coast's planned neighborhoods and steady new construction are well-suited to FHA — modern homes within the loan limit, ideal for first-time buyers. Our dedicated Palm Coast city guide covers neighborhoods and local down payment help in depth. Pair FHA with Florida assistance and gift funds to keep your upfront cost low.

Communities We Serve

FHA loans across Flagler County

We help FHA buyers throughout Flagler County, including Palm Coast, Bunnell, and Flagler Beach. For a closer look at the local market, loan limits, and down payment help, explore our city guide:

Palm Coast

Down payment help in Flagler County

You don't have to cover the down payment alone. FHA allows gift funds, sellers can contribute up to 6% toward closing costs, and Flagler County buyers can often pair FHA with assistance from the Florida Housing Finance Corporation and Flagler County's local SHIP-funded programs. Funding and eligibility change frequently, so we'll help you confirm what's currently available — see our FHA down payment assistance guide.

Questions & Answers

Frequently asked questions

What is the FHA loan limit in Flagler County for 2026?+
Flagler County is at the 2026 Florida FHA floor of $541,287, which covers nearly all homes in the county. Confirm the exact figure with HUD's official lookup.
Is Flagler County good for FHA buyers?+
Yes — affordability and steady new construction in Palm Coast make it a strong fit for first-time and relocating FHA buyers.
Does FHA work for new-construction homes in Palm Coast?+
Yes — newer homes typically pass the FHA appraisal easily and sit within the loan limit.
How much down payment do I need in Flagler County?+
As little as 3.5% with a 580+ score, reduced with gift funds and Florida assistance.
Do you serve all of Flagler County?+
Yes — Palm Coast, Bunnell, Flagler Beach, and every community in the county.
